name: Williamsburg-James City County Public Schools
searchAliases: Williamsburg James City County Public Schools
districtFact: Williamsburg-James City County Public Schools publishes school year calendar PDFs with first day, last day, marking period starts, family conference days, CLP days, early releases, winter break, spring break, and make-up day notes.

about.title: About Williamsburg-James City County Public Schools
about.content: Williamsburg-James City County Public Schools, commonly called WJCC Schools, is a public school division serving Williamsburg and James City County, Virginia, in the Historic Triangle and Hampton Roads region. The district calendar follows a traditional school-year format for PK-12 families across elementary, middle, and high school schedules. It lists first day, last day, holidays, family conference days, student holidays, staff CLP days, marking period starts, winter break, spring break, early release days, and report card dates.

about.title: How to Use This WJCC Calendar
about.content: Use this page for districtwide school-year planning across elementary, middle, and high school schedules. Families should confirm school-specific events, bell schedules, early release times, program calendars, make-up day changes, and graduation details through WJCC Schools and their assigned school.

planningTips.title: Planning Tips for WJCC Families
content: Track CLP days separately because some affect all students and some affect only PK-5 students.
Check early release dates by grade span because the calendars distinguish PK-12, K-12, and grades 9-12 release days.
Use family conference days as no-school planning dates for childcare.
Review the official WJCC calendar page for PDF revisions and superintendent-directed make-up day changes.

customSections.label: About WJCC Schools
customSections.content: Williamsburg-James City County Public Schools, commonly called WJCC Schools, serves students in Williamsburg and James City County, Virginia. The school division supports PK-12 families across elementary, middle, and high school schedules in the Historic Triangle and Hampton Roads region. Families use the WJCC academic calendar to plan attendance, transportation, childcare, school activities, family conferences, holidays, CLP days, early releases, and school-year breaks. Because some official calendar dates are separated by grade span, parents should also confirm school-specific schedules with their assigned campus.

groups.label: Local Calendar Context
items: Connects the WJCC calendar to Williamsburg, James City County, and the Hampton Roads region.
Explains why CLP days, family conferences, and grade-span early releases matter for family planning.
Clarifies that school-specific bell schedules, programs, and make-up day changes should still be verified with WJCC Schools.

customSections.label: Planning Guide for WJCC Families
customSections.content: The WJCC Schools calendar is most useful when families treat it as both a date reference and a planning tool. Parents can use the districtwide dates to plan childcare, transportation, appointments, family travel, and school activities around holidays, CLP days, early releases, and conference dates. Winter Break and Spring Break are the easiest travel windows to identify, but family conference days, PK-5 CLP days, and grade-span early releases often have the biggest effect on weekday logistics. Students can use the calendar to track breaks, marking period changes, and the final weeks of the school year. Teachers and staff may use the same calendar to coordinate planning days, professional learning, grade-span early releases, and family communication. Because some WJCC dates apply differently to PK-5, K-12, grades 9-12, or all students, families should read each label carefully and confirm school-specific bell schedules with their assigned school.

groups.label: For Parents and Guardians
items: Use Winter Break, Spring Break, family conference days, and student holidays to plan childcare and travel.
Check early release labels before scheduling afternoon appointments or transportation changes.
Confirm campus-level bell schedules and program calendars before making final arrangements.
Watch PK-5 CLP days separately because they may affect elementary families differently from middle and high school families.

groups.label: For Students
items: Track marking period starts, breaks, and the final school week to plan assignments and activities.
Pay attention to grade-span early releases because high school and elementary schedules may differ.
Use Spring Break and the final marking period dates as checkpoints for projects, exams, and end-of-year activities.

groups.label: For Teachers and School Staff
items: Use CLP days, family conference dates, and marking period transitions as planning checkpoints.
Refer families back to WJCC Schools for official updates, make-up day decisions, and revised calendars.
Coordinate family communication around student holidays and early release dates that can be confused with full districtwide closures.

customSections.label: Related WJCC and Virginia School Calendars
customSections.content: Use these related pages to compare WJCC school years and nearby Virginia district schedules.

compareIntro: Compare the Williamsburg-James City County school calendar with other Virginia district calendars currently available on MySchoolDates. Dates shown include the first day, last day, and spring break.

districtFaqs.q: Which WJCC calendar does this page summarize?
districtFaqs.a: This page summarizes the official Williamsburg-James City County Public Schools school year calendar PDFs for the listed school years.
Yes. The calendars include staff CLP days, including PK-5-only student holidays in some years.

districtFaqs.q: Does WJCC distinguish early release by grade span?
districtFaqs.a: Yes. The official calendars label early releases for PK-12, K-12 with PK student holiday, and grades 9-12 depending on the date.

districtFaqs.q: Where should families verify WJCC calendar updates?
districtFaqs.a: Use the official WJCC Schools calendar page and school announcements for final confirmation. MySchoolDates is an independent reference and is not affiliated with WJCC Schools.

districtFaqs.q: Does the WJCC calendar apply to every school?
districtFaqs.a: The district calendar covers districtwide WJCC dates, but individual schools may publish school-specific events, bell schedules, activities, and program calendars.
